content.full_text:
May 7, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street (8th Floor)
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Sirs:

Brenda Marin, resident of Irwindale, has filed suit against Patricio Miranda, formerly Vice Mayor of Irwindale and Vice Chairman of the Irwindale Community Redevelopment Agency, alleging that Miranda violated Statute 1090. Miranda is currently an Irwindale City Councilman and a Commissioner of the Irwindale Community Redevelopment Agency. The lawsuit was filed by Marin on behalf of the Irwindale Community Redevelopment Agency in the Pomona Division of the Los Angeles Superior Court as EAC 74402. Miranda has filed a cross-complaint against me in the same action.

The only parties to this lawsuit are three private individuals, namely Ms. Marin, Mr. Miranda, and myself. The Irwindale CRA previously has declined to file a 1090 action against Miranda. In spite of this, the Irwindale CRA has been paying Miranda's legal bills and Miranda, as Vice Chairman of the Irwindale CRA, has voted to approve such payments.

A ruling is respectfully requested from your office on the following questions:

1) Does the payment of Miranda's legal bills by the Irwindale CRA constitute an illegal gift of public funds?

2) Does the action of Miranda in voting for the CRA to pay his bills violate Statute 1090?

3) Is the payment of Miranda's legal bills by the CRA an action which the CRA is mandated by law to do or is it a discretionary action by the CRA?

Sincerely,

Frederick P. Lyte

FPL:dc
cc: Pete McCloskey, Esquire
David Kays, Esquire
